name: Gacha Pull
description: A paid or earned random reward draw from a published pool, often with rarity tiers and duplicate handling.
design_notes: Gacha pulls are high-risk economy mechanics because cost, odds, duplicates, pity, and presentation affect player trust. The design should make eligibility, rarity, duplicate_rule, and pity_counter behavior explicit before a pull is committed.
design_purpose: Create anticipation around rare rewards while controlling long-term reward distribution.
Support collection goals through banners, pools, duplicate conversion, and pity rules.
Give designers a tunable reward sink for earned or premium currency without hiding the rules.
player_fantasy: The player feels the suspense of a draw while believing the reward rules are understandable and fair.

parameters.name: banner_id
parameters.description: Identifier for the active reward pool, featured items, pity rules, and availability window.

parameters.name: pull_cost_minor_units
parameters.description: Currency cost for one draw, stored in minor units for exact debits.

parameters.name: rarity_tier_weights
parameters.description: Relative odds for each rarity tier before pity or guarantee modifiers.

parameters.name: pity_counter_threshold
parameters.description: Pull count after which a protected rarity or featured reward is guaranteed or boosted.

parameters.name: duplicate_rule
parameters.description: Rule applied when the selected reward is already owned or exceeds inventory limits.

edge_cases: Currency is debited but reward grant fails because inventory is full or the item definition is missing.
A banner expires between preview and commit, so the server must reject or honor the request consistently.
A duplicate reward triggers conversion and pity progress in the same pull.
Cross-device play can submit the same pull transaction_id twice if the first response times out.
common_bugs: Pity counters increment on preview or failed pulls instead of committed reward grants.
Client-side RNG selects the reward and can be manipulated or desynced from server logs.
Published odds differ from configured rarity_tier_weights after banner filters are applied.
Duplicate conversion grants shards but also leaves the duplicate item_instance_id in inventory.
balancing_notes: Simulate expected pulls to featured rewards, worst-case pity outcomes, and duplicate conversion value before pricing the banner.
If real money can influence pulls, transparency and regional compliance matter as much as reward tuning.
Duplicate_rule and pity_counter settings should reduce frustration without making the highest rarity feel routine.
accessibility_notes: Show odds, pity progress, duplicate conversion results, and spending confirmation in readable text.
Allow long animations to be skipped or reduced, especially for repeated multi-pulls.

implementation_notes.unity: Treat a pull as a server-authoritative transaction_id: debit currency, roll banner_id with deterministic logged seed, apply duplicate_rule and pity_counter, grant item_instance_id, then animate the committed result.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep banner definitions in Resources or JSON and separate the reward commit from reveal animation; local-only games should still persist pity_counter and transaction history before showing rewards.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use an economy service or backend-backed subsystem for pull authority; replicated clients should receive committed reward payloads rather than rolling in UMG or Blueprints.
implementation_notes.web: For browser games, execute the roll in a transaction reducer or backend endpoint, store pull history and pity_counter, and render reveal UI from the committed reward list.

example_games.title: Genshin Impact
example_games.note: Uses banners and guarantee-style rules for character and weapon draws.

example_games.title: Fire Emblem Heroes
example_games.note: Uses character summoning pools with rarity outcomes.
